greater_than_comparison.label: Numeric Comparisons and Boundaries
greater_than_comparison.summary: Choose strict or inclusive comparison operators for numeric thresholds.

gt-sc-meaning.title: What does > mean?
gt-sc-meaning.prompt: In a SQL WHERE clause, what does `price > 20` mean?
gt-sc-meaning.hint: Think about whether rows equal to 20 are included.

help.concept: The `>` comparison operator keeps rows where the column value is strictly larger than the value on the right side.
help.hint_1: Ask whether a row with `price = 21` should stay and whether a row with `price = 20` should stay.
help.hint_2: This operator is strict: it keeps values above 20, not values equal to 20.

options.a: Keep rows where price is less than 20
options.b: Keep rows where price is greater than 20
options.c: Keep rows where price is exactly 20
options.d: Sort rows by price from highest to lowest

gt-sc-inclusive.title: Does > include the boundary?
gt-sc-inclusive.prompt: A product has `stock = 15`. Will it match `WHERE stock > 15`?
gt-sc-inclusive.hint: Compare exact equality with a strict greater-than check.

help.concept: A strict greater-than comparison excludes rows where the value is exactly the same as the boundary number.
help.hint_1: The row would need a stock value larger than 15, not the same as 15.
help.hint_2: If the condition were `>= 15`, equality would count, but this condition is different.

options.a: Yes, because 15 is part of greater than 15
options.b: Yes, but only if the row is in the Home category
options.c: No, because 15 is not larger than 15
options.d: No, because WHERE cannot compare numbers

gt-mc-matching-prices.title: Rows that match price > 20
gt-mc-matching-prices.prompt: Choose all products from the lesson dataset that match `WHERE price > 20`.
gt-mc-matching-prices.hint: Check each product price and keep only values above 20.

help.concept: To evaluate `price > 20`, compare each row's price to 20 and include only rows with a larger number.
help.hint_1: Prices above 20 in the dataset include 24.50, 39.99, and 129.00.
help.hint_2: Rows priced 12.99, 6.75, and 18.00 do not qualify because they are not above 20.

gt-mc-valid-where-clauses.title: Valid greater-than filters
gt-mc-valid-where-clauses.prompt: Choose all WHERE clauses that correctly use `>` to filter the `products` table by a numeric column.
gt-mc-valid-where-clauses.hint: Look for numeric columns in this dataset, such as price and stock.

help.concept: A greater-than comparison is commonly used with numeric columns to keep rows above a threshold.
help.hint_1: In this table, `price` and `stock` are numeric columns that work naturally with `>`.
help.hint_2: Clauses using equality or a text-only comparison are not examples of the greater-than numeric filter asked for here.

gt-order-query-parts.title: Build a greater-than query
gt-order-query-parts.prompt: Arrange the SQL parts to create a query that shows product names with price greater than 20.
gt-order-query-parts.hint: Start with SELECT, then FROM, then WHERE.

help.concept: A basic filtering query follows the order SELECT columns, FROM table, then WHERE condition.
help.hint_1: The condition `price > 20` belongs after the table name because it is part of the WHERE clause.
help.hint_2: Put the column list first, then the source table, then the filter.

gt-order-evaluate-stock.title: Reason through stock > 10
gt-order-evaluate-stock.prompt: Put these steps in order for checking whether a row matches `WHERE stock > 10`.
gt-order-evaluate-stock.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

tokens.t3: Keep the row only if the value is larger

gt-fb-price-threshold.title: Complete the price filter
gt-fb-price-threshold.prompt: Fill in the missing value to keep products with price greater than 20.
gt-fb-price-threshold.hint: The blank should be the number used as the comparison boundary.

help.concept: In `WHERE price > value`, the value on the right sets the minimum threshold that rows must exceed.
help.hint_1: You want the condition to mean 'more than twenty'.
help.hint_2: Choose the numeric value that makes the clause read `price > ...` for products above the blank.

gt-fb-price-threshold.template: SELECT name FROM products WHERE price > [blank1];

gt-fb-column-choice.title: Complete the stock query
gt-fb-column-choice.prompt: Fill in the missing column name so the query keeps products with stock greater than 10.
gt-fb-column-choice.hint: Use the numeric column that counts how many items are available.

help.concept: The left side of a greater-than comparison should be the column whose numeric values you want to test.
help.hint_1: In the `products` table, the quantity-on-hand column is not `price` or `category`.
help.hint_2: Choose the column that stores values like 25, 10, 8, 50, 15, and 3.

try-greater_than_comparison-sketch1.title: Include the Restock Boundary
try-greater_than_comparison-sketch1.prompt: A promotion requires at least `15` units in stock, and items exactly on the boundary must count. Return `name, stock` from `products` in that column order. Keep only rows where `stock >= 15`.
try-greater_than_comparison-sketch1.hint: At least means use >= so 15 remains in the result.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `name, stock` from `products` in that column order. Keep only rows where `stock >= 15`.

try-greater_than_comparison-sketch1.starterCode:
SELECT name, stock
FROM products
-- Keep stock of 15 or more

try-greater_than_comparison-sketch2.title: Find Stock Above a Strict Threshold
try-greater_than_comparison-sketch2.prompt: Find products that have more than `20` units available for a large-order list. Return `id, name, stock` from `products` in that column order. Keep only rows where `stock > 20`.
try-greater_than_comparison-sketch2.hint: Use > because a stock value of exactly 20 should not be included.

help.concept: Read the brief as a result contract: identify the source table or join path, the result columns, the row condition or grouping rule, and any required ordering.
help.hint_1: Build the result one clause at a time and run it after each meaningful change.
help.hint_2: Return `id, name, stock` from `products` in that column order. Keep only rows where `stock > 20`.

try-greater_than_comparison-sketch2.starterCode:
SELECT id, name, stock
FROM products
-- Keep stock strictly above 20

try_greater_than_comparison_sketch2.title: Find Stock Above a Strict Threshold
try_greater_than_comparison_sketch2.prompt: Find products that have more than `20` units available for a large-order list. Show `id`, `name`, and `stock` from `products`.
try_greater_than_comparison_sketch2.hint: Use > because a stock value of exactly 20 should not be included.

help.concept: A strict comparison excludes the boundary value itself.
help.hint_1: Use > because a stock value of exactly 20 should not be included.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the returned columns, rows, and order with the expected result.

try_greater_than_comparison_sketch1.title: Include the Restock Boundary
try_greater_than_comparison_sketch1.prompt: A promotion requires at least `15` units in stock, and items exactly on the boundary must count. Show `name` and `stock` from `products` that qualify.
try_greater_than_comparison_sketch1.hint: At least means use >= so 15 remains in the result.

help.concept: An inclusive comparison keeps rows at the boundary as well as rows above it.
help.hint_1: At least means use >= so 15 remains in the result.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the returned columns, rows, and order with the expected result.

gt-code-price-over-20.title: Query products priced above 20
gt-code-price-over-20.prompt: Write a query that shows the `name` and `price` of all products whose `price` is greater than 20. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
gt-code-price-over-20.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

gt-code-price-over-20.starterCode:
-- Show the name and price of all products whose price is greater than 20.
SELECT name, price
FROM products
WHERE ;

gt-code-stock-over-10.title: Query products with stock above 10
gt-code-stock-over-10.prompt: Write a query that shows the `name` and `stock` columns for products whose `stock` is greater than 10. Use the SQL editor, click Run query, and inspect the results table.
gt-code-stock-over-10.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

gt-code-stock-over-10.starterCode:

SELECT name, stock
FROM products

gt-code-home-price-over-30.title: Filter Home products above 30
gt-code-home-price-over-30.prompt: Write a query that shows `name`, `category`, and `price` for products in the `Home` category whose `price` is greater than 30. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
gt-code-home-price-over-30.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

gt-code-home-price-over-30.starterCode:
-- Show name, category, and price for products in the Home category whose price is greater than 30.
SELECT name, category, price
FROM products
WHERE category = 'Home'
  AND ;

strict_numeric_comparisons.title: Strict Comparisons Exclude the Boundary
strict_numeric_comparisons.bodyMarkdown:
`>` and `<` are strict comparisons. The boundary value itself does not pass.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    price
FROM products
WHERE price > 25;
```

A product priced exactly `25` is excluded because the request says greater than, not greater than or equal to. Likewise, `price < 25` excludes exactly `25`.

Underline words such as “more than,” “above,” “less than,” and “below” before choosing the operator.

inclusive_boundaries.title: Inclusive Comparisons Keep the Boundary
inclusive_boundaries.bodyMarkdown:
`>=` and `<=` include the boundary.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    price
FROM products
WHERE price <= 20;
```

A price of exactly `20` passes. These operators match phrases such as “at most 20,” “20 or less,” “at least 10,” and “10 or more.”

When a filter seems off by one boundary row, compare the requirement with the operator. The difference between `<` and `<=` is small in syntax but important in meaning.
